metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC3 the exact test mechanism for the sdistâ†’wheel path: the test must invoke `python -m build --no-isolation` (or the equivalent subprocess) with `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` set, then inspect the resulting wheel's `_build_info.py` for the exact SHA â€” and state whether this lives in `tests/unit/` or a new `tests/integration/` location.
findings.finding: AC3's proving test is placed in `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py` but the plan does not specify whether the test invokes `python -m build` end-to-end (the production sdistâ†’wheel subprocess path) or only exercises the Hatchling hook's env-var reading in isolation â€” these two test shapes prove entirely different things, and the plan's own text says the test must 'run REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=<short-hex> python -m build and assert the resulting wheel carries that exact short SHA'.
findings.location: AC3; What section ('Verify env-var propagation'); Scope â€” `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'The test invokes `subprocess.run(["python", "-m", "build", "--no-isolation"], env={**os.environ, "REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT": "abc1234"}, ...)`, extracts the built wheel with `zipfile`, and asserts `_build_info.COMMIT == "abc1234"`. This test may live in `tests/integration/` to separate it from fast unit tests.'

findings.finding: AC3 says 'a test asserts the wheel built via the default sdistâ†’wheel path contains COMMIT equal to that SHA' and assigns it to `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py`, but the body explicitly warns 'rather than asserting propagation as settled fact' and demands a test that 'runs `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=<40-char-hex> python -m build`'. A unit test in that file exercising a direct `WheelBuilder` call (the current pattern in lines 202-227) can satisfy the AC letter without ever invoking `python -m build` end-to-end, leaving the sdistâ†’wheel subprocess path untested â€” exactly the defect the plan aims to close.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria â€” AC3; What section â€” 'Verify env-var propagation' bullet; `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the AC3 proving clause: 'A test (may live in `tests/integration/` or as a separate fixture) invokes `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=<40-char-hex> python -m build --no-isolation --outdir <tmpdir>` in a temp directory, unpacks the resulting `.whl`, imports `_build_info`, and asserts `COMMIT == <sha>`; a direct WheelBuilder call does NOT satisfy this criterion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the sdist-test job sets `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` to the release short SHA (passed as a job output from the build job or extracted from the artifact bundle) before building the wheel from the sdist, so the non-null-COMMIT assertion in AC2 can pass without a `.git` tree present.
findings.finding: The sdist `include` configuration in `pyproject.toml` (`include = ["src/rebar", "tests", "README.md"]`) covers tracked files; `_build_info.py` is git-ignored and therefore excluded from the sdist by default. The wheel target's `artifacts` list explicitly force-includes `src/rebar/_build_info.py`, but the sdist target has no parallel `artifacts` entry. This means the sdist shipped in the bundle will not contain a baked `_build_info.py`. The sdist-test job in AC2 asserts 'the installed package reports the intended SHA' after building a wheel from the sdist â€” this assertion relies entirely on REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT propagating into the sdistâ†’wheel build during the test job, which requires the test job to also set that env var with the correct SHA. The plan does not specify that the sdist-test job sets `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT`.
findings.location: AC2 (sdist-test job); Scope bullet 'pyproject.toml â€” adjust force-include / build config if needed'; pyproject.toml [tool.hatch.build.targets.sdist]

findings.finding: The plan's new fail-fast rule â€” 'the hook raises (fails the build) only when REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is set but empty/unresolvable' â€” introduces a decision protocol into hatch_build.py that is never fully specified. 'Empty' is clear (the env var is set to ''), but 'unresolvable' is undefined: the env var's value is not passed to any resolver (it is used directly as the SHA string), so 'unresolvable' has no obvious meaning unless the plan intends validation (e.g. must be 40-char hex). The hook's current code writes whatever commit value it obtains without validation, and the plan does not define what constitutes an invalid non-empty value.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Fail-fast scoped to the release context' bullet; AC #3 â€” 'The hook fails the build only when REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is set but unresolvable'; hatch_build.py:initialize()
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Fail-fast' bullet: 'Validation: if REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is set, the hook checks that its value matches the regex ^[0-9a-f]{40}$ (a full SHA); any other non-empty value (e.g. empty string, "undefined", partial hex) raises BuildError("REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT must be a 40-char hex SHA, got: {value!r}"). An unset env var (os.environ.get returns None) skips validation and falls back to git.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the cross-story 'guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`' claim with an explicit prerequisite: either (a) list `--no-isolation` (or `python -m build -n`) as a required change in THIS story's scope and add it to the release.yml edit, or (b) add an AC gate that the new sdist-test job (not just the new unit test) empirically demonstrates the baked SHA arrives via the sdistâ†’wheel path before this story is closed.
findings.finding: The plan states as settled fact that env-var propagation of `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` through `python -m build` into the Hatchling `initialize` hook (during the sdistâ†’wheel sub-build) 'is guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`', but the current `release.yml` (line 38) uses bare `python -m build` with no `--no-isolation` flag, and Finding 4 is a separate, undelivered story â€” its `--no-isolation` addition is not in scope for this plan. The guarantee rests on an unverifiable cross-story assertion.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Fix the hook' bullet: 'On the release path this is guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `--no-isolation` (equivalently `-n`) to the `python -m build` invocation in this story's `release.yml` changes, and document it as part of this story's scope rather than depending on Finding 4. The sdist-test job AC already proves propagation empirically when the wheel from the sdist carries the correct SHA.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the env-var propagation guarantee within this story's own scope: either (a) add `--no-isolation` to the `python -m build` invocation in this plan's `release.yml` changes (not relying on Finding 4), or (b) add a `pyproject.toml` `[tool.hatch.envs.build]` config that passes `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` explicitly to the backend, or (c) replace the env-var mechanism with a `hatch_build.py`-native solution (e.g. write a `PKG-INFO`-aware SHA reader from the sdist metadata) that does not depend on isolated subprocess env-var inheritance; and add the proving test that runs `python -m build` end-to-end and asserts the resulting wheel's `COMMIT` equals the injected value.
findings.finding: The plan's core fix â€” `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` reaching the Hatchling `initialize()` hook during `python -m build`'s sdistâ†’wheel sub-build â€” rests on an unverified capability: that the PEP 517 isolated subprocess (an ephemeral venv spawned by `pypa/build`) inherits the host shell's env vars. The plan simultaneously asserts this is 'guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`' and warns 'do not assume' / 'the plan must still prove it with a test.' These are contradictory, and `--no-isolation` is owned by a separate concurrent plan (Finding 4) not in scope here â€” so there is no guarantee within this story's deliverables. If the build runs in default isolated mode without `--no-isolation`, `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` may not reach the hook, and the shipped wheel will still carry `COMMIT = None`.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Fix the hook' and 'Verify env-var propagation' bullets; AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add `--no-isolation` (the correct flag name for `pypa/build`, not `--no-build-isolation`) to the `python -m build` invocation in this story's `release.yml` build step, making this story self-contained and not dependent on Finding 4's delivery. Update the proving test to use the same flag. This both fixes the propagation gap and removes the cross-story dependency.

findings.finding: The env-var propagation guarantee ('guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with --no-isolation') is stated as a settled fact in the 'What' section, but Finding 4 (pinning story) is explicitly scoped as a *separate* story with only 'coordinate' mentioned in this plan's Scope. If this story is implemented without Finding 4, PEP 517 build isolation will block REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT from reaching the Hatchling hook inside the extracted sdist build, silently yielding COMMIT=None again â€” the exact defect being fixed.
findings.location: What section ('Verify env-var propagation') and Scope section ('coordinate with Findings 1 and 4')
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add `--no-isolation` to this story's own release.yml changes and AC1 (removing the dependency on Finding 4 for correctness), or (b) add an AC like 'The build step uses --no-isolation or an equivalent mechanism that allows REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT to reach the Hatchling hook' and make Finding 4 a stated prerequisite gate.

findings.finding: The plan asserts as settled fact that 'on the release path [env-var propagation] is guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`', but no `--no-isolation` flag exists in any current workflow file, and the referenced 'Finding 4' plan document is not accessible to verify this cross-story dependency. This is a committed-work dependency on an unverifiable cross-story assertion.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Verify env-var propagation' paragraph: 'on the release path this is guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with --no-isolation (no PEP 517 subprocess isolation at all)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit `python -m build --no-isolation` (or `python -m build -n`) to the build step in this plan's release.yml changes, or add a test that verifies the env var reaches the hook through the default isolated build mode (e.g., by running `python -m build` without `--no-isolation` in a temp dir with the env var set and checking the resulting wheel).

findings.finding: The plan's core fix â€” `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` reaching the Hatchling `initialize()` hook during the sdistâ†’wheel sub-build â€” rests on an unverified capability assertion about `pypa/build`'s env-var propagation through PEP 517 isolation. The plan simultaneously acknowledges uncertainty ('do not assume') and asserts it is 'guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`' â€” but `--no-isolation` is Finding 4's scope, not this story's, making the guarantee circular and ungrounded at design time.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Fix the hook' / 'Verify env-var propagation' subsection
findings.suggested_fix: Add `--no-isolation` (or `-n`) to `python -m build` in the build job spec within this story's scope (it does not need to wait for Finding 4), and cite the pypa/build source or docs confirming that with `--no-isolation` the backend runs in-process / inherits env. Then the propagation guarantee is locally grounded and testable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the `github_release` job as a consumer of the artifact bundle and explicitly state whether `SHA256SUMS` should be included or excluded from the GitHub Release assets (e.g., update the `gh release upload` glob or confirm the SHA256SUMS inclusion is intentional).
findings.finding: The `github_release` job in `release.yml` (line 75â€“107) downloads the artifact bundle with `name: dist` and uploads `dist/*` to the GitHub Release. After this plan adds a `SHA256SUMS` file to the bundle, `dist/*` will include `SHA256SUMS` as a GitHub Release asset. The plan's Scope section does not enumerate `github_release` as a consumer requiring review or deliberate treatment of the new file.
findings.location: Scope section / .github/workflows/release.yml github_release job (lines 75-107)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Scope bullet: '`.github/workflows/release.yml` `github_release` job â€” confirm/update the `dist/*` glob to include or exclude `SHA256SUMS` from the GitHub Release assets (recommend including it for supply-chain transparency).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) add an explicit prerequisite stating 'this story requires --no-isolation in the release.yml build step (from Finding 4) and must not merge without it', or (b) add --no-isolation as a deliverable of this story's release.yml changes, with an AC verifying the flag is present in the build step.
findings.finding: The env-var propagation guarantee ('guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with --no-isolation') is a cross-story dependency: this story's own scope and ACs do not require `--no-isolation` to be established or validated independently, so if Finding 4 is not yet merged when this story lands, the propagation guarantee silently fails and no AC in this story would catch it.
findings.location: What section ('Verify env-var propagation'); Scope â€” `.github/workflows/release.yml`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope bullet for release.yml: 'Ensure the build step uses --no-isolation (coordinate with Finding 4; if Finding 4 is not yet merged, this story adds the flag).' Add a corresponding AC: 'The release.yml build step invokes python -m build --no-isolation so that REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is visible to the Hatchling hook when building the wheel from the extracted sdist.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the env-var propagation rationale: replace the claim that `--no-isolation` (Finding 4) is required for propagation with the accurate statement that `pypa/build` inherits host env vars into the Hatchling subprocess even in isolated mode â€” the proving test (AC3) is the verification, not `--no-isolation`.
findings.finding: The plan anchors the env-var propagation guarantee to `--no-isolation` from a separate concurrent story (Finding 4), but this rationale is mechanically incorrect: `pypa/build` inherits host environment variables into the backend subprocess even in its default isolated mode (isolation creates a clean venv but the subprocess call inherits the parent env). The stated dependency on Finding 4's `--no-isolation` flag means implementers may believe this story cannot ship independently, or may add `--no-isolation` as a hard requirement in this story's scope when it is not needed for env-var propagation. The actual propagation guarantee comes from `pypa/build`'s subprocess semantics, not from `--no-isolation`.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Fix the hook' bullet: 'On the release path this is guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`'
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace: 'On the release path this is guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation` (no PEP 517 subprocess isolation at all)'
With: 'On the release path this works because `pypa/build` inherits the host process environment into the backend subprocess even in isolated mode (isolation creates a clean venv but the subprocess call inherits the parent env, so `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` reaches `initialize()`). The proving test in AC3 â€” running `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=<short-hex> python -m build` and asserting the resulting wheel carries that exact SHA â€” demonstrates propagation empirically rather than relying on this reasoning.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit prerequisite/dependency guard in Scope or AC3: either (a) this plan itself adds `--no-isolation` to the `python -m build` invocation in `release.yml` (making it a deliverable here rather than relying on Finding 4), or (b) add an AC that verifies `--no-isolation` is present in the final workflow file, with a note that Finding 4 is a hard prerequisite that must land first.
findings.finding: The What section asserts that env-var propagation into the Hatchling hook is 'guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`', but `--no-isolation` is owned by a separate plan (Finding 4) and does not appear as a deliverable, dependency guard, or prerequisite in this plan's Scope or ACs. If Finding 4 is not landed (or is modified), the core mechanism of this plan silently breaks â€” yet AC3 only asserts the end-result SHA value, not that `--no-isolation` is present.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Verify env-var propagation' sub-bullet; Scope â€” '.github/workflows/release.yml'; AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Move the `--no-isolation` flag ownership into this plan's Scope for `release.yml` (it is directly required by the env-var injection mechanism), and add a line to AC3: 'The build command uses `--no-isolation` so that `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` is inherited by the Hatchling hook when the wheel is built from the extracted sdist.' This removes the implicit dependency on Finding 4 for correctness of this plan's core mechanism.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) scope adding `--no-isolation` (or equivalent PEP 517 env-passthrough config) to the `python -m build` invocation explicitly into THIS plan's release.yml changes and AC, or (b) declare Finding 4 as a hard prerequisite with a sequencing constraint and add an AC assertion that verifies `--no-isolation` is present before the env-var fix is considered complete.
findings.finding: The plan's env-var fix (REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT propagating into the Hatchling hook during the sdistâ†’wheel build) silently depends on `--no-isolation` being present in the `python -m build` invocation, which is owned by Finding 4, not this plan. The plan acknowledges this in a note ('Finding 4 builds with --no-isolation') but neither scopes the flag into this ticket's release.yml changes nor makes it a hard AC or prerequisite. If this plan is implemented independently of Finding 4, the env var will not propagate through PEP 517 subprocess isolation, so the wheel still ships COMMIT = None â€” the exact bug being fixed.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Verify env-var propagation (do not assume)'; Scope â€” '.github/workflows/release.yml'; AC line 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope entry for release.yml: 'add `--no-isolation` to the `python -m build` invocation (or confirm Finding 4 has already applied it â€” gate this story on that finding).' Add to AC 3: 'The `python -m build` command uses `--no-isolation` so that REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is visible to the Hatchling hook during the sdistâ†’wheel sub-build.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the unverified capability assertion about `pypa/build`'s env-var forwarding behavior: explicitly confirm (in this plan's scope, not deferred to Finding 4) the exact flag name required (`--no-isolation`, not `--no-build-isolation`) and add it to this story's `release.yml` build step, so the env-var propagation guarantee is owned here rather than cross-story â€” then verify it empirically with the `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=<short-hex> python -m build` test AC#3 mandates.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that env-var propagation from the workflow shell into the Hatchling `initialize()` hook during the sdistâ†’wheel sub-build is 'guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`', but this relies on an unverified capability assertion about how `pypa/build` handles its PEP 517 subprocess environment in isolated vs. non-isolated modes â€” and the flag name itself (`--no-isolation` vs. `--no-build-isolation`) is contested across prior review cache entries for sibling stories, making the guarantee's load-bearing dependency unresolved within this plan.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Fix the hook (decision: inject the SHA via an env var)' bullet; 'Verify env-var propagation (do not assume)' bullet; AC#3
findings.suggested_fix: In the build step of `release.yml`, explicitly include `--no-isolation` (the correct `pypa/build` flag, verified against `python -m build --help`) in the `python -m build` invocation within this story's scope rather than depending on Finding 4. Add a unit test in `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py` that runs `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=deadbee python -m build --no-isolation` against a temp directory and asserts the resulting wheel contains `COMMIT = 'deadbee'` â€” this is the proof the plan already mandates in AC#3 but does not yet anchor to a specific flag.

findings.finding: The plan states env-var propagation into the Hatchling hook during the sdistâ†’wheel sub-build is 'guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`', but this plan's own scope and ACs contain no explicit prerequisite edge on Finding 4, no statement that `--no-isolation` will be added by this story, and no fallback if Finding 4 is not applied concurrently. An executor implementing this story independently would encounter propagation failure without knowing why.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Verify env-var propagation (do not assume)' bullet; Scope â€” `.github/workflows/release.yml`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet to Scope: 'This story adds `--no-isolation` to the `python -m build` invocation in `release.yml`; Finding 4 is a hard prerequisite (or this story subsumes that flag).' Then update AC3 to mandate: 'A dedicated test invokes `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=<sha> python -m build --no-isolation` in a temp dir, extracts the resulting wheel, and asserts `COMMIT == <sha>` â€” not a direct WheelBuilder call.'

findings.finding: The env-var propagation guarantee is load-bearing but cross-story: the plan states propagation is 'guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`', yet `--no-isolation` is not in this plan's scope. If this story lands before Finding 4, or if the two stories are decoupled, `python -m build` runs in default isolated mode and `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` may not reach the Hatchling `initialize()` hook during the sdistâ†’wheel sub-build â€” exactly the failure mode the plan is trying to fix. The plan explicitly says 'do not assume â€” prove it with a test', but the proving test only asserts the RESULT (wheel carries the SHA), not the precondition (isolation mode). A mid-flight landing order where this story's hook change is live but Finding 4's `--no-isolation` is not yet merged would silently produce `COMMIT = None` again.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Verify env-var propagation (do not assume)' bullet; Scope â€” 'coordinate with Findings 1 and 4'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `--no-isolation` to the `python -m build` call in this plan's release.yml build step (the plan already notes pypa/build inherits env vars when isolation is disabled), making the mechanism self-contained. The Scope section already touches release.yml, so this is within scope. The AC #3 test that asserts the wheel-from-sdist path should also be updated to run with `--no-isolation` so it validates the same code path used in the release workflow.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit ordering / co-deployment guard: either (a) require `--no-isolation` to be present in the same PR as the env-var fix (enforced by a single atomic change to `release.yml`), or (b) change the fail-fast rule so the hook also raises when `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` is unset AND the resulting git lookup yields `None` (covering the isolation-stripped case), so no silent degradation path exists in the release workflow.
findings.finding: The env-var propagation fix has a silent-failure dependency on Finding 4's `--no-isolation` flag: the plan states the approach is 'guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`', but if that story is not yet applied or is later reverted, `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` is swallowed by PEP 517 subprocess isolation and the hook silently falls back to `git rev-parse` inside the extracted sdist (which has no `.git`), regenerating `COMMIT = None` with no build-time error because the env var is unset (not 'set but empty/unresolvable').
findings.location: What section â€” 'Fix the hook' + 'Verify env-var propagation'; Scope â€” `hatch_build.py` fail-fast rule
findings.suggested_fix: Change the hook logic to: if REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is set â†’ use it (fail if empty); elif git lookup succeeds â†’ use git result; elif we are in a build triggered by the release workflow (detectable via another env var, e.g. GITHUB_ACTIONS=true) â†’ raise; else degrade gracefully. This makes the fail-fast cover the isolation-stripped case without breaking dev installs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide and state explicitly in the Scope bullet whether the `test.yml` artifact probe is IN or OUT of this story's done criteria (e.g. 'out of scope for this story; tracked as follow-on'), and if in-scope, add a corresponding AC.
findings.finding: The `test.yml` scope bullet says 'optionally add a wheel/sdist artifact probe to regular CI' without deciding whether it is required for this story's done state, deferring the inclusion/exclusion decision to the executor.
findings.location: Scope â€” `.github/workflows/test.yml` bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'optionally add a wheel/sdist artifact probe to regular CI' with either: (a) 'OUT OF SCOPE â€” tracked as follow-on ticket' or (b) add it as a required scope item and add a matching AC such as 'test.yml regular CI runs the wheel install smoke test on every push'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the publish job handles a duplicate-upload scenario (e.g., `twine upload --skip-existing` or an explicit check for the version's existence on PyPI before uploading) so a job retry after a successful-but-transient-error upload does not permanently block the release.
findings.finding: The publish job has no idempotency guard for PyPI re-upload: if the GitHub Actions job is retried after a successful PyPI publish (network blip, transient GHA failure), the `twine upload` / `gh release upload` will hit a 400 'File already exists' error with no graceful handling specified, which can leave the release workflow in a permanently-failed state even though the artifact is already live.
findings.location: Scope / release.yml â€” publish job; Acceptance Criteria bullet 1 ('publishes without rebuilding')
findings.suggested_fix: Add `--skip-existing` to the `twine upload` invocation and document in `docs/releasing.md` that a re-run after a partial publish is safe; alternatively, add a pre-upload step that checks `pip index versions <package>` and skips upload if the version already exists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test case in `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py` (or a new unit test) that sets `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` to an empty string (or an invalid value) and asserts that the Hatchling `initialize` hook raises an exception, covering the fail-fast branch.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `hatch_build.py` should fail the build when `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` is set but empty/unresolvable (release-context fail-fast), but no test is scoped to verify this failure path. The testing scope only adds coverage for the 'wheel-from-sdist path (the currently-untested COMMIT = None case)' â€” which is the happy path of the new behavior â€” leaving the fail-fast branch untested.
findings.location: Scope â€” 'tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py'; AC line 3; What section â€” 'Fail-fast scoped to the release context'
findings.suggested_fix: In the test file, add a parametrized case: `@pytest.mark.parametrize('bad_val', ['', ' '])` that monkeypatches `os.environ['REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT']` to the bad value and calls the hook's `initialize` method, asserting it raises `ValueError` (or similar).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge the `${GITHUB_SHA::7}` vs `git rev-parse --short` length divergence risk (or show it is negligible for this repo's size), and specify whether `hatch_build.py` should accept the env-var value as-is or normalize it to match `--short`'s output length (e.g. by calling `git rev-parse --short <REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT>` when the env var is set).
findings.finding: The plan asserts that '`signing.py` is unchanged' and that 'its short-SHA `_gate_commit_sha` path already matches the chosen canonical short format, so the baked value and the live-git value stay consistent in `gate_code_version()` output regardless of install type.' However, `git rev-parse --short` produces variable-length SHAs (7 chars by default, but longer when needed for uniqueness in large repos), while `${GITHUB_SHA::7}` is always exactly 7 characters. These two can produce different strings for the same commit in repos with hash collisions at 7 chars â€” yet the plan treats them as equivalent without verifying.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Canonical commit format = SHORT SHA' bullet; AC3 â€” 'the baked value and `signing.py`'s live-git value share the short format'

findings.finding: Workflow re-run idempotency is unaddressed: if the build job is re-triggered (e.g., transient runner failure forces a retry), it produces a new artifact upload under the same name, potentially with different SHA-256 hashes (non-deterministic build timestamps in wheel metadata). Test jobs that already passed against the original artifact hashes would have validated bytes that differ from the re-built artifact, breaking the build-once guarantee and making the SHA256SUMS gate meaningless on re-runs.
findings.location: ## What â€” 'Publish the same bytes' bullet; Acceptance Criteria AC-1
findings.suggested_fix: Key the artifact name with `${{ github.run_id }}` (e.g., `rebar-dist-${{ github.run_id }}`); test and publish jobs reference the same run-scoped name, ensuring they always consume the artifact from their own build job and a re-run produces a new, self-consistent chain rather than overwriting a previously-validated artifact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `mcp_registry` (and `github_release`) to the Scope section as downstream consumers of the restructured `publish` DAG, confirming their `needs: publish` chains remain correct and acknowledging the wall-clock latency increase.
findings.finding: The `mcp_registry` job's `needs: publish` chain is not enumerated as a consumer requiring review. The plan says `publish` will now `needs:` both `wheel-test` and `sdist-test` jobs, extending the DAG depth. While the structural dependency chain (`mcp_registry â†’ publish â†’ [wheel-test, sdist-test] â†’ build`) is implicitly correct, the `mcp_registry` job is not mentioned in the Scope section, leaving the executor to discover the DAG impact independently.
findings.location: Scope section / .github/workflows/release.yml mcp_registry job (lines 109-179)

findings.finding: The plan claims 'any `signing.py` display/match code is confirmed to accept the full SHA', but `signing.py` is built around SHORT SHAs throughout: `_gate_commit_sha` calls `git rev-parse --short HEAD`, its docstring says 'Short commit SHA'; `gate_code_version`'s docstring says `<short-sha>[-dirty]`; `rebar_version_step`'s docstring says `<short-sha>[-dirty]`. The plan proposes changing `hatch_build.py`'s git fallback to full SHA but does not mention changing `signing.py`'s live-git path â€” creating a format skew where the baked SHA (full 40-char) differs from the live-checkout SHA (short) in the same `rebar-version:` manifest line format.
findings.location: What section â€” 'Canonical commit format = full 40-char SHA' paragraph: 'any `signing.py` display/match code is confirmed to accept the full SHA'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/signing.py` to the Scope section; change `_gate_commit_sha` to call `git rev-parse HEAD` (no `--short`) and update its docstring. Alternatively, keep both paths on short SHAs and change the env-var value to a short SHA (e.g., `${{ github.sha | cut -c1-7 }}`), but that breaks the 'confirmed 40-char' claim.

findings.finding: The plan changes `hatch_build.py` to bake a full 40-char SHA (from `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` / `git rev-parse HEAD`) but does not include `src/rebar/signing.py` in the Scope, even though `_gate_commit_sha()` there still calls `git rev-parse --short HEAD` â€” meaning a wheel-installed rebar on a git-present machine would mix a full-SHA baked value (from `_baked_commit_sha()`) with a short-SHA live git value (from `_gate_commit_sha()`), producing inconsistent `gate_code_version()` output depending on install type.
findings.location: What section ('Canonical commit format = full 40-char SHA') / src/rebar/signing.py:300-305 / Scope section

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
